context.setting: Working on laptop with laptop on Right

episode_text:
day=3; meal=bite-sized pizza and broccoli; setting=Working on laptop with laptop on Right; time_of_day=noon;
preferences: microwave_time=2 min; occlusion_relevance=do not consider occlusion; robot_speed=slow; skewering_axis=parallel to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=no; transfer_mode=outside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=near; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=button; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=button; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=button; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=button; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=button; retract_between_bites=no; bite_dipping_preference=do not dip; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=10 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF food_condition is non-cold food without sauces/dips in a quiet personal setting with neutral affect THEN 2 min BECAUSE this was selected in the observed neutral non-cold meals and no context-dependent variation in reheating has been observed. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF dining context is quiet personal AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is non-sauce food THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE the user accepted no special view-preservation behavior in the personal meal. IF dining context involves attending to something on the user's left (e.g., watching TV on the left) AND affective_state is neutral THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE the user explicitly chose view preservation aligned with the location of the attended object. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is neutral THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE the user did not request right-side view preservation in that work context.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF dining context is quiet personal AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is non-sauce food THEN slow BECAUSE the user selected a comfort-oriented movement speed in the personal meal. IF dining context is watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food includes a dip THEN slow BECAUSE the user again chose the slowest speed. IF dining context is working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is non-sauce food THEN slow BECAUSE the user maintained the same comfort-first speed while working. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF feeding food requires fork acquisition AND user has limited mouth opening THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE narrower bites are more compatible with the user's physical constraint, and this was selected across observed meals. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF dining context is quiet personal AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is typical solid food THEN no BECAUSE the user chose to avoid the extra confirmation step. IF dining context is watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food includes a dip THEN no BECAUSE the user again preferred faster interaction without explicit bite-acquisition confirmation. IF dining context is working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is non-sauce food THEN no BECAUSE the user continued to prefer efficiency over explicit confirmation while multitasking. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF dining context is quiet personal AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is typical solid food THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user can lean forward comfortably and selected the less intrusive transfer style. IF dining context is watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food includes a dip THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user maintained the same less intrusive transfer style. IF dining context is working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is non-sauce food THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user again preferred the less intrusive style. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND dining context is quiet personal AND affective_state is neutral THEN near BECAUSE the user prefers the robot to stop close to the mouth while still requiring self-initiated bite taking. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND dining context is watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral THEN near BECAUSE the user kept the same close stopping distance in a distraction-sensitive context. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND dining context is working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is neutral THEN near BECAUSE the user again kept the close stopping distance while multitasking. IF transfer_mode is inside mouth transfer THEN not applicable BECAUSE this parameter only applies to outside-mouth transfer. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is quiet personal AND affective_state is neutral THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user selected redundant multimodal signaling in the personal meal. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral THEN LED BECAUSE the user switched to a less verbally intrusive visual-only cue while attending to external media. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is neutral THEN LED BECAUSE the user again preferred visual-only signaling while engaged with a screen, indicating a tendency to reduce speech interruptions during media or work contexts.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF task is feeding AND dining context is quiet personal AND affective_state is neutral THEN button BECAUSE the user has reliable arm control, can press buttons easily, and limited mouth opening makes open-mouth readiness less attractive. IF task is feeding AND dining context is watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral THEN button BECAUSE the user again preferred explicit manual control, consistent with avoiding ambiguous open-mouth detection while distracted. IF task is feeding AND dining context is working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is neutral THEN button BECAUSE the user maintained explicit manual initiation while multitasking. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

IF task is drinking AND dining context is quiet personal AND affective_state is neutral THEN button BECAUSE the user can reliably provide explicit input and selected direct control over sip initiation. IF task is drinking AND dining context is watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral THEN button BECAUSE the user again preferred explicit initiation. IF task is drinking AND dining context is working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is neutral THEN button BECAUSE the user maintained explicit initiation while working. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

IF task is mouth wiping AND dining context is quiet personal AND affective_state is neutral THEN button BECAUSE the user can reliably press buttons and selected explicit control for wipe initiation. IF task is mouth wiping AND dining context is watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral THEN button BECAUSE the user again preferred explicit initiation. IF task is mouth wiping AND dining context is working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is neutral THEN button BECAUSE the user maintained explicit initiation while multitasking. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is quiet personal AND affective_state is neutral THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user preferred clear multimodal notification when the tool reached transfer position. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral THEN LED BECAUSE the user switched to visual-only completion signaling, suggesting a preference for less verbal interruption when attending to media. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is neutral THEN LED BECAUSE the user again preferred visual-only completion signaling while engaged with a screen.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF task is feeding AND dining context is quiet personal AND affective_state is neutral THEN perception BECAUSE the user accepted the reliable automatic completion detection for bites. IF task is feeding AND dining context is watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral THEN perception BECAUSE the user again preferred reliable automatic bite completion detection. IF task is feeding AND dining context is working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is neutral THEN perception BECAUSE the user maintained the same reliable automatic completion method while multitasking. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF task is drinking AND dining context is quiet personal AND affective_state is neutral THEN button BECAUSE the user chose explicit completion signaling rather than automatic head-gesture-based detection. IF task is drinking AND dining context is watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral THEN button BECAUSE the user again preferred explicit manual completion. IF task is drinking AND dining context is working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is neutral THEN button BECAUSE the user maintained explicit manual completion while multitasking. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF task is mouth wiping AND dining context is quiet personal AND affective_state is neutral THEN button BECAUSE the user chose explicit completion signaling for wiping. IF task is mouth wiping AND dining context is watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral THEN button BECAUSE the user again preferred explicit manual completion. IF task is mouth wiping AND dining context is working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is neutral THEN button BECAUSE the user maintained explicit manual completion while working. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF dining context is quiet personal AND affective_state is neutral THEN no BECAUSE the user accepted keeping the robot nearby between bites, consistent with prioritizing efficiency in a personal setting. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral THEN yes BECAUSE the user chose to retract between bites to reduce prolonged visual obstruction while attending to media. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is neutral THEN no BECAUSE the user did not request retraction in that work context, suggesting retraction is not a general response to all screen-based activities.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF food_condition is no sauce or no dippable item THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping is unnecessary and this was the observed choice in non-dip meals. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context involves watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral THEN less BECAUSE the user preferred some dipping while limiting messiness in a distraction-sensitive context.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF dining context is quiet personal AND affective_state is neutral THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user selected the shortest available wait, consistent with efficient progression in a low-distraction setting. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the user preferred a longer intervention window while attention was partly occupied by external media. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is neutral THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user kept the shortest wait while working, indicating that longer waits are not preferred in all multitasking contexts.
